name: Pike in Galentyne
recipeInstructions:
1. Put the fish in a pan, add the wine, vinegar, parsley stalks, and enough salted water to cover the fish.
2. Gently poach over low heat for 15 minutes. Turn off the heat; if needed, the fish will finish cooking as it cools.
3. Cover the pan and cool to tepid before finishing the dish.
4. Carefully lift the fish out of the pan and reserve the cooking liquid.
5. Place bread slices in a bowl and add enough of the reserved liquid to cover them.
6. Remove the skin, spine, and bones from the fish; pike has thin bones running through its flesh on each side. Cut all the flesh into small pieces for easier handling.
7. Strain the remaining cooking liquid into a clean pan.
8. Add 425 ml/15 fl oz/2 cups of strained liquid to an electric blender with soaked bread, cinnamon, and white pepper; blend until smooth.
9. Return the mixture to the liquid in the pan. Fry onions in oil until soft and add them to the liquid.
10. Season to taste, then reheat gently before serving.
11. For a cold dish, keep fish pieces and fried onions aside while measuring and tasting the liquid. Add enough gelatine to stiffen it (if cooked whole, it would jell naturally). Return fish pieces and onions, turn into a mold, and refrigerate until set.
